topics.name: Introduction to the Zoo and Observation Skills
topics.summary: Introduces the concept of a zoo through visual observation and basic questions, including a listening comprehension activity.

topics.name: Zoo Vocabulary, Alphabet Practice, and Rhyme
topics.summary: Activities focused on identifying zoo-related vocabulary, practicing alphabet recognition and writing, and a singing/dancing rhyme.

topics.name: Alphabet Sequencing and Writing
topics.summary: Students practice alphabet sequencing by joining dots from A to Z, identify and write capital and small letters, and color a picture.

topics.name: Identifying and Naming Zoo Animals and Birds
topics.summary: Students read animal names, identify animals not present in a given list, write names of animals and birds, associate them with their first letters, and classify them.

topics.name: Abhi goes to Zoo Story and Comprehension
topics.summary: A short story about Abhi's journey to the zoo with his classmates, followed by visual comprehension activities.

topics.name: Animal Action Song and Creative Writing
topics.summary: Students engage in singing and dancing to a song about animal actions and are encouraged to add new lines to the song, fostering creativity and vocabulary.

topics.name: Picture Story Narration: A Mouse and a Snake
topics.summary: Students interpret a four-panel picture story about a mouse and a snake and narrate it orally to their peers, developing visual comprehension and storytelling skills.

topics.name: Introduction to the Story
topics.summary: Students observe pictures and answer questions to introduce the characters and setting of the story 'Where are you, Sweety?' and engage in a listening activity.

topics.name: The Story: Where are you, Sweety?
topics.summary: Students read a short story about Abhi and Meena searching for their kitten, Sweety, and their father helping to retrieve it from a tree.

topics.name: Story Comprehension and Personal Connection
topics.summary: Students answer various questions to demonstrate their understanding of the story, identify key details, and relate the story to their own experiences with pets.

topics.name: Creative Expression and Writing
topics.summary: Students engage in creative activities involving drawing, coloring, and writing, inspired by elements from the story.

topics.name: Animal Vocabulary and Word Search
topics.summary: Students identify various animals and locate their names within a word search grid, enhancing their vocabulary and recognition skills.

topics.name: Animal Babies and Colours
topics.summary: This topic introduces students to the names of common animal babies and provides exercises for identifying and colouring them. It also includes activities to practice identifying and writing colour names.

topics.name: Drawing and Creative Writing
topics.summary: This topic guides students through a step-by-step process of drawing an animal and encourages them to name their drawing and write a short description about it, fostering creativity and writing skills.

topics.name: Singing and Song Extension
topics.summary: This topic presents a simple song about 'My little Sweety' and encourages students to extend the song by adding new lines about other animals, promoting listening, singing, and creative writing skills.

topics.name: The Journey to Uncle's House
topics.summary: This topic introduces Siddu and his father's journey to his uncle's house, encountering various modes of transport and challenges along the way, culminating in his arrival and joy at seeing puppies.

topics.name: Post-Reading Activities
topics.summary: This topic contains various exercises designed to test comprehension, vocabulary, and writing skills based on the story 'A Visit to Uncle's House'.

topics.name: Vehicles and Modes of Transport
topics.summary: This topic introduces students to different types of vehicles, their modes of travel, and associated sounds and actions through identification, drawing, maze activities, and a song.

topics.name: Storytelling from Pictures
topics.summary: This topic focuses on developing oral storytelling skills by describing a sequence of events depicted in pictures.

topics.name: Introduction to the Story (Picture Analysis)
topics.summary: Students analyze pictures related to Siddu and Snoopy going to school and answer questions, including a listening comprehension activity.

topics.name: The Story: Snoopy Comes to School
topics.summary: The main story about Snoopy searching for Siddu in school, visiting different classes, and finally finding him.

topics.name: Story Comprehension and Application
topics.summary: Exercises to test understanding of the story, correct statements, and identify school-related items.

topics.name: Creative Activity: Drawing and Colouring
topics.summary: An art activity involving colouring a school scene and drawing a flag.

topics.name: About My School
topics.summary: Activities focused on describing one's school environment, including personal details and identifying various places within the school.

topics.name: Abilities and Actions
topics.summary: Learning to ask and answer questions about personal abilities using 'Can you?' in a conversational context.

topics.name: Food and Feelings
topics.summary: Activities related to identifying different food items and expressing various emotions.

topics.name: Creative Expression and Rhyme
topics.summary: Engaging with a song/poem through singing, dancing, and creative writing by adding new lines.

topics.name: Story Time: A Mistake
topics.summary: Reading and comprehending a short story about a common everyday mistake.

topics.name: Look at the picture and answer the question given below.
topics.summary: Students observe a detailed illustration of a street scene during rainfall, identifying elements and activities.

topics.name: Look at the picture and answer the questions given below. / Your teacher will tell you what happened on a rainy day. Listen carefully and answer the following questions.
topics.summary: Students observe a picture of a boy looking out a window during rain and answer questions. They then listen to a story about a rainy day and answer comprehension questions, including personal reflection.

topics.name: Read the following.
topics.summary: Students read a short descriptive text about rain and observe illustrations of children playing in the rain, answering comprehension and personal reflection questions.

topics.name: Meena's Rainy Day Story
topics.summary: Students read a story about Meena's desire to play in the rain, her mother's refusal due to a cold, and Meena's clever solution to join the fun with an umbrella, answering comprehension questions.

topics.name: Do the following.
topics.summary: Students complete fill-in-the-blank exercises to reinforce vocabulary and comprehension from the chapter, answer open-ended questions, and describe activities in a new rainy day picture.

topics.name: Rainy Day Activities Survey
topics.summary: Students survey their friends about various activities they did on a rainy day and record the responses in a table.

topics.name: Meena's Rainy Day Conversation
topics.summary: A short story about Meena forgetting her umbrella and getting wet, followed by a role-play activity to complete a conversation between Meena and her mother.

topics.name: I Am The Winner! Word Game
topics.summary: A listening and writing game where students select words from a list, write them down, and cross them out as the teacher calls them, aiming to be the first to cross out all their words.

topics.name: Number-Letter Code Breaker
topics.summary: Students decode sentences by replacing numbers with corresponding letters from a provided alphabet key.

topics.name: Rainy Season Project Work
topics.summary: Students draw and color pictures of two items used during the rainy season and discuss them.

topics.name: Rain on the Green Grass Song
topics.summary: Students sing and dance to a poem about rain and add new lines to it.

topics.name: Introduction to Games and Observation
topics.summary: Students observe pictures of children playing various games and answer questions based on their observations, including a listening activity related to games.

topics.name: The Story: Here is your coin!
topics.summary: A narrative about children playing cricket, where one child finds a lost coin, and the group eventually returns it to its rightful owner, emphasizing honesty and empathy.

topics.name: Story Comprehension and Application
topics.summary: Exercises designed to assess students' comprehension of the story 'Here is your coin!' through multiple-choice and short-answer questions, followed by an activity to list different games.

topics.name: Identifying Playthings and Personal Preferences
topics.summary: Students identify various playthings, specifically cricket equipment, and express their personal preferences by drawing and writing about their favorite play items.

topics.name: Conversation and Kite Making
topics.summary: Students engage in a role-play conversation about making a kite and identify the materials needed for kite making.

topics.name: Social Skills and Project Work
topics.summary: Students learn to respond appropriately to social situations during play and undertake a project to research and write about a favorite sports player.

topics.name: Song and Creative Writing
topics.summary: Students engage in singing and dancing to a given song and then creatively add new lines to the song.

topics.name: Introduction and Initial Observation
topics.summary: This topic introduces the chapter through picture observation and initial questions, setting the stage for the story about a fly searching for its name.

topics.name: The Story: What's My Name?
topics.summary: This topic presents the narrative of a fly searching for its name, encountering various people engaged in different professions.

topics.name: Comprehension and Application of Story Concepts
topics.summary: This topic includes activities designed to test comprehension of the story and apply learned vocabulary about professions.

topics.name: Word Search and Dialogue Completion
topics.summary: This section includes a word search activity to find words from the chapter text and a dialogue completion exercise based on the story characters.

topics.name: Creative Activities and Community Helpers
topics.summary: This section involves a colouring activity and a project to research tools used by different community helpers.

topics.name: Rhyme and Movement: Bang and a bang!
topics.summary: This section features a song/poem about a cobbler, encouraging students to sing and dance along.

topics.name: Story Time: Three Little Kittens
topics.summary: This section presents a new story about three kittens and their adventures, followed by a vocabulary building sidebar on animal babies.

topics.name: Introduction to the Story / Picture Analysis
topics.summary: Students observe a picture related to the chapter theme and answer questions based on their observation and interpretation.

topics.name: Identifying Ingredients for Carrot Halwa
topics.summary: Students read a list of items and select the necessary ingredients for making carrot halwa, based on previous story context.

topics.name: Creative Expression: Favourite Vegetables
topics.summary: Students draw and colour their favourite vegetables and write descriptive sentences about them.

topics.name: Project Work: Favourite Sweets Survey
topics.summary: Students conduct a simple survey among their friends to find out their favourite sweets and record the information in a table.

topics.name: Vegetable and Social Interaction Songs
topics.summary: Students sing poems about vegetables and participate in a song with actions about sharing and helping.

topics.name: Chapter 8 Narrative Summary: Healthy Eating and Growing Carrots
topics.summary: A narrative summary of Chapter 8, focusing on Appu's desire for carrot halwa after a birthday party, his mother's advice on the health implications of eating too many sweets, and the suggestion to grow fresh carrots in their garden for daily consumption. This reinforces themes of healthy eating and the origin of food.

topics.name: Textbook Development and Publication Information
topics.summary: Details the teams involved in the development and publication of the textbook, including the Textbook Development Team, Textbook Publication Team, and QR Code Team.

topics.name: Notes Section
topics.summary: Provides blank lined pages for students or teachers to write notes, observations, or additional information.

topics.name: Digital Content Access (QR Codes)
topics.summary: Instructions on how to use QR codes and the DIKSHA platform to access digital content linked to the textbook, including videos, lessons, and documents.

topics.name: Personal Safety Education
topics.summary: Educates students on personal body safety rules, including identifying private parts, understanding appropriate and inappropriate touch, and knowing how to respond and seek help.

topics.name: Childline Support Services
topics.summary: Information about the Childline Foundation (1098), a service provided by the Government of Telangana for children in need of help or protection from dangers and problems.

topics.name: Emergency Services Contact
topics.summary: Provides information about dialing 100 for emergency services, specifically mentioning Telangana Police.

topics.name: Vegetable Identification and Naming
topics.summary: Students identify and name various vegetables from pictures, using a word bank.

topics.name: Role Play: Sweet Shop Conversation
topics.summary: Students engage in a group role-play activity simulating a conversation in a sweet shop.

topics.name: Introducing Carrot Halwa and Listening Comprehension
topics.summary: Students observe a picture and dialogue about halwa, answer questions, and prepare for a listening activity about the boy and carrot halwa.

topics.name: The Story: Appu Tries to Pull a Carrot
topics.summary: Introduction to the story of Appu, who plants carrots and struggles to pull out a very large one, eventually calling for help.

topics.name: The Story: More Friends Join to Pull the Carrot
topics.summary: The story continues with Appu asking Meena for help, and then Abhi joining them, but they still cannot pull out the giant carrot.

topics.name: The Story: Success Through Teamwork
topics.summary: The story concludes with Snoopy the dog joining the effort, and together, the group successfully pulls out the giant carrot.

topics.name: Story Conclusion and Comprehension
topics.summary: The story concludes with Appu's mother making carrot halwa from the giant carrot, which everyone enjoys. Followed by comprehension questions about the story.

topics.name: Post-Reading Activities and Group Discussion
topics.summary: Activities to reinforce comprehension of the story and encourage creative thinking and discussion about carrots and food.

subtopics.name: Observing a Zoo Scene
subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture of children on a toy train at a zoo with various animals.

subtopics.name: Answering Questions based on Observation
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions based on their observation of the zoo scene.

subtopics.name: Observing a Travel Scene
subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture of a boy running after a school van.

subtopics.name: Listening Comprehension
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story about a boy named Abhi and answer related questions.

subtopics.name: Identifying Objects in a Picture
subtopics.summary: Students identify and circle specific words corresponding to objects or animals found in the zoo pictures.

subtopics.name: Writing Zoo-related Words
subtopics.summary: Students write names of animals, birds, and things from the zoo pictures into an alphabetical table.

subtopics.name: Alphabet Song and Dance
subtopics.summary: Students engage in a singing and dancing activity based on an alphabet rhyme.

subtopics.name: Alphabet Sequencing (A-Z dot-to-dot)
subtopics.summary: Students connect dots labeled with letters from A to Z in sequence to reveal a picture.

subtopics.name: Capital and Small Letter Writing
subtopics.summary: Students write capital and small letters of the English alphabet in provided tables.

subtopics.name: Picture Colouring
subtopics.summary: Students color the picture revealed by the dot-to-dot activity.

subtopics.name: Reading Animal Names
subtopics.summary: Students read a list of animal names presented in a cloud graphic.

subtopics.name: Animal Name Recall and Writing
subtopics.summary: Students recall and write the names of animals seen in earlier pages that were not listed in the provided cloud.

subtopics.name: Animal/Bird Name and First Letter Association
subtopics.summary: Students write the names of various animals and birds, along with their corresponding first letters (capital and small), and color their pictures.

subtopics.name: Animal/Bird Classification and Naming
subtopics.summary: Students categorize and write the names of different animals and birds found in a zoo using a mind map structure.

subtopics.name: Reading the Story 'Abhi goes to Zoo'
subtopics.summary: Students read a short narrative about Abhi's trip to the zoo.

subtopics.name: Answering Questions about the Zoo
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions based on the visual content of the zoo pictures.

subtopics.name: Visualizing the Zoo Environment
subtopics.summary: Students look at pictures depicting Abhi and his classmates inside the zoo, observing various animals.

subtopics.name: Singing and Movement
subtopics.summary: Students sing and dance along to a song about animals and their actions.

subtopics.name: Creative Songwriting
subtopics.summary: Students create and add new lines to the existing song, describing other animal actions.

subtopics.name: Picture Interpretation
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a sequence of images to understand the events and plot of a story.

subtopics.name: Oral Story Narration
subtopics.summary: Students verbally recount the story depicted in the pictures to their classmates.

subtopics.name: Picture Observation and Discussion
subtopics.summary: Students analyze an image of a family and a kitten, answering questions to infer the initial plot.

subtopics.name: Character Interaction and Listening Comprehension
subtopics.summary: Students observe images of characters interacting and answer questions, followed by a listening activity to understand the context of 'Sweety'.

subtopics.name: Searching for Sweety
subtopics.summary: Students read about Abhi and Meena looking for Sweety inside their house, specifically under the cot and on the cupboard.

subtopics.name: Finding Sweety in the Garden
subtopics.summary: Students read about Abhi and Meena finding Sweety in a tree in the garden and their father, Mohan, helping to bring her down.

subtopics.name: Multiple Choice Comprehension
subtopics.summary: Students answer multiple-choice questions to recall specific details from the story, such as character names and the kitten's location.

subtopics.name: Location Identification
subtopics.summary: Students identify and mark the places where Abhi and Meena searched for Sweety.

subtopics.name: Open-ended Comprehension and Personal Reflection
subtopics.summary: Students answer open-ended questions about the story and reflect on their own experiences with pets.

subtopics.name: Drawing and Coloring Fruits
subtopics.summary: Students draw and color their favorite fruit on a depicted 'magic tree' where Sweety was found.

subtopics.name: Writing Fruit Names
subtopics.summary: Students write the names of other fruits they enjoy.

subtopics.name: Animal Identification and Word Search
subtopics.summary: Students identify common animals from pictures and find their names hidden in a letter grid.

subtopics.name: Identifying Animal Mothers and Babies
subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify various animal mothers and their corresponding babies through pictures and a word box.

subtopics.name: Colouring and Colour Recognition
subtopics.summary: Students practice colouring skills by matching colours to animal pairs and a boy's dress. They also recall and write names of colours they know.

subtopics.name: Writing Colour Names
subtopics.summary: Students engage in a mind-mapping activity to list and write down various colour names.

subtopics.name: Step-by-Step Drawing
subtopics.summary: Students follow a sequence of steps to draw a cat, developing their fine motor skills and artistic ability.

subtopics.name: Naming and Describing a Drawing
subtopics.summary: Students are prompted to give a name to their drawn cat and write a few sentences about it, encouraging imaginative thinking and basic writing practice.

subtopics.name: Singing 'My little Sweety'
subtopics.summary: Students learn and sing a short song about a kitten, developing listening and oral language skills.

subtopics.name: Adding Lines to a Song
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to create and add new verses to the existing song, using given prompts for other animals like a ducky and a puppy, enhancing their creative writing and rhyming abilities.

subtopics.name: Observing and Inferring from a Scene
subtopics.summary: Students observe a detailed picture of a crowded street and answer questions that require visual interpretation and basic inference about the scene and characters' intentions.

subtopics.name: Predicting and Listening Comprehension
subtopics.summary: Students predict why Siddu won't come to school based on a speech bubble and then listen to a story segment (read by the teacher) to answer specific comprehension questions about Siddu's trip.

subtopics.name: Reading the Beginning of the Story
subtopics.summary: Students read the initial part of the narrative, learning about Siddu's excitement and the start of his journey to his uncle's house by cart and then bus.

subtopics.name: Reading About the Journey's Obstacle
subtopics.summary: Students read about an unexpected event during the journey – a bus tyre burst – and Siddu's subsequent sadness and the family's attempt to find alternative transport.

subtopics.name: Reading About Reaching the City
subtopics.summary: Students read about Siddu and his father continuing their journey, encountering more challenges with transport, and finally reaching the bustling city of Hyderabad.

subtopics.name: Reading About the Arrival and Joy
subtopics.summary: Students read about Siddu's arrival at his uncle's house, being greeted by his relatives, and his immense happiness upon discovering and playing with puppies.

subtopics.name: Identifying Vehicles and Travel Environments
subtopics.summary: Students identify various vehicles (boat, car, bus, airplane) and categorize them by where they travel (air, water, road).

subtopics.name: Drawing and Coloring Vehicles
subtopics.summary: Students practice drawing and coloring a bus and a car, following step-by-step instructions for the car.

subtopics.name: Vehicle Maze and Counting
subtopics.summary: Students solve a maze involving cars, count the number of cars, and determine which cars can exit and which exits last.

subtopics.name: Vehicle Song and Sounds
subtopics.summary: Students sing a song about vehicles (bus, train, car, bicycle), their movements, and sounds, and add new lines to the song.

subtopics.name: Describing Sequential Pictures
subtopics.summary: Students observe a series of four pictures showing a dog's interaction with a glass of water and narrate the story.

subtopics.name: Analyzing pictures and answering questions
subtopics.summary: Students observe images and answer questions based on visual cues and inference.

subtopics.name: Listening comprehension about Snoopy and Siddu
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story or dialogue (from Appendix-1) and answer questions based on the audio.

subtopics.name: Reading the story
subtopics.summary: Students read the narrative text about Snoopy's adventure at school.

subtopics.name: Understanding the narrative sequence
subtopics.summary: Students follow the sequence of events in the story, such as Snoopy visiting different classes.

subtopics.name: Identifying characters and their emotions
subtopics.summary: Students recognize characters like Snoopy and Siddu and understand their feelings throughout the story.

subtopics.name: Answering direct questions about the story
subtopics.summary: Students answer open-ended and inferential questions based on the story content.

subtopics.name: Choosing correct answers from options
subtopics.summary: Students select the appropriate answer from given choices to demonstrate story comprehension.

subtopics.name: Correcting false statements from the story
subtopics.summary: Students identify incorrect statements related to the story and rewrite them correctly.

subtopics.name: Identifying and naming school-related items
subtopics.summary: Students recognize items typically found in a school bag and write their names.

subtopics.name: Colouring a given image
subtopics.summary: Students colour parts of a school building image according to instructions.

subtopics.name: Drawing an element in an image
subtopics.summary: Students draw a flag on a flag post within the school image.

subtopics.name: Describing My School
subtopics.summary: Students fill in blanks to provide personal information about their school.

subtopics.name: Identifying School Places
subtopics.summary: Students identify and list different areas or facilities found within a school.

subtopics.name: Understanding Dialogue
subtopics.summary: Students read and comprehend a short dialogue between characters about their capabilities.

subtopics.name: Asking and Answering 'Can you?' Questions
subtopics.summary: Students practice asking and responding to questions about various actions and abilities.

subtopics.name: Identifying Food Items
subtopics.summary: Students list various food and drink items based on visual cues.

subtopics.name: Expressing Emotions
subtopics.summary: Students identify and select the appropriate emotional response to a given scenario.

subtopics.name: Singing and Reciting
subtopics.summary: Students participate in singing and dancing to a short poem.

subtopics.name: Creative Writing
subtopics.summary: Students extend a poem by adding new rhyming lines based on provided prompts.

subtopics.name: Reading a Short Story
subtopics.summary: Students read a narrative text to understand the plot and characters.

subtopics.name: Understanding the Moral/Context
subtopics.summary: Students grasp the context and humorous resolution of the story.

subtopics.name: Visual observation of a rainy street
subtopics.summary: Students identify and describe elements present in a picture depicting a rainy street with people and houses.

subtopics.name: Visual observation of a boy in the rain
subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture of a boy looking out a window during rain and infer his potential actions.

subtopics.name: Listening comprehension of a rainy day story
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story narrated by the teacher about a rainy day and answer specific questions based on the narrative.

subtopics.name: Personal reflection on rainy day activities
subtopics.summary: Students share their personal preferences and activities related to rainy days.

subtopics.name: Reading descriptive text about rain
subtopics.summary: Students read and understand simple sentences describing where and how rain falls.

subtopics.name: Comprehension of children's activities in rain
subtopics.summary: Students identify and describe various activities children are engaged in while playing in the rain, such as floating paper boats and splashing.

subtopics.name: Expressing enjoyment and preferences for rain
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on their own enjoyment of rain and how they like to play in it.

subtopics.name: Reading and understanding Meena's story
subtopics.summary: Students read a narrative about Meena and her interaction with rain, including dialogue and actions.

subtopics.name: Comprehension of character actions and motivations
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions about Meena's feelings, her mother's reasons, and Meena's solution to play in the rain.

subtopics.name: Vocabulary and sentence completion
subtopics.summary: Students choose appropriate words to complete sentences related to the chapter's content.

subtopics.name: Answering comprehension questions
subtopics.summary: Students provide written answers to questions based on the stories and themes of the chapter.

subtopics.name: Picture description and personal opinion
subtopics.summary: Students describe actions in a given picture of children playing in the rain and express their personal likes and reasons.

subtopics.name: Surveying Friends
subtopics.summary: Students ask friends if they participated in specific rainy day activities.

subtopics.name: Recording Responses
subtopics.summary: Students mark a tick in a table for each friend who did a particular rainy day activity.

subtopics.name: Story Comprehension
subtopics.summary: Students read and understand a short story about Meena's experience on a rainy day.

subtopics.name: Conversation Completion
subtopics.summary: Students complete a dialogue between Meena and her mother based on the rainy day story.

subtopics.name: Role Play
subtopics.summary: Students act out the completed conversation in groups.

subtopics.name: Word Selection and Writing
subtopics.summary: Students choose six words from a given list and write them in boxes.

subtopics.name: Listening and Recognition
subtopics.summary: Students listen to words read by the teacher and identify if they have written them down, then cross them out.

subtopics.name: Decoding Sentences
subtopics.summary: Students use a number-to-letter key to fill in blanks and complete sentences.

subtopics.name: Reading Decoded Sentences
subtopics.summary: Students read the sentences after successfully decoding them.

subtopics.name: Drawing Rainy Season Items
subtopics.summary: Students draw two objects commonly used in the rainy season.

subtopics.name: Coloring Drawings
subtopics.summary: Students color their drawings of rainy season items.

subtopics.name: Discussing Drawings
subtopics.summary: Students talk about the items they drew and colored.

subtopics.name: Singing and Dancing
subtopics.summary: Students sing a poem about rain and perform actions or dance.

subtopics.name: Adding New Lines to Poem
subtopics.summary: Students compose and add new lines to the 'Rain on the green grass' poem.

subtopics.name: Observing children playing cricket
subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture of children playing cricket and answer questions about the scene.

subtopics.name: Observing children playing various games
subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture showing children engaged in multiple games and answer questions about their activities.

subtopics.name: Listening to a song about games
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a song about playing games and answer questions related to their own experiences with games.

subtopics.name: Reading the beginning of the story
subtopics.summary: Students read the initial part of the story, introducing characters playing cricket and the first event.

subtopics.name: Reading about Teja finding the coin
subtopics.summary: Students read about Teja's actions after the ball is hit, leading to the discovery of a five-rupee coin.

subtopics.name: Reading about the coin's ownership and Manu crying
subtopics.summary: Students read the dialogue among the children regarding the coin's ownership and the discovery of Manu crying.

subtopics.name: Reading the resolution of the story
subtopics.summary: Students read how the coin is identified as Manu's and returned, concluding the story.

subtopics.name: Multiple choice questions on the story
subtopics.summary: Students answer multiple-choice questions based on the events and characters in the story.

subtopics.name: Short answer questions on the story
subtopics.summary: Students provide short written answers to questions requiring deeper understanding of the story's plot and themes.

subtopics.name: Listing different games
subtopics.summary: Students recall and write the names of various games children play, applying their knowledge of the chapter's theme.

subtopics.name: Identifying Playthings
subtopics.summary: Students observe pictures of various playthings.

subtopics.name: Identifying Cricket Gear
subtopics.summary: Students identify and list items specifically used for playing cricket from a collection of playthings.

subtopics.name: Drawing Favorite Playthings

subtopics.name: Writing about Favorite Playthings
subtopics.summary: Students write a few sentences describing one of their drawn favorite playthings.

subtopics.name: Role-playing a Conversation
subtopics.summary: Students role-play a conversation between two friends about making a kite.

subtopics.name: Completing a Dialogue
subtopics.summary: Students complete a given dialogue based on the context of making a kite.

subtopics.name: Identifying Kite Making Materials
subtopics.summary: Students identify and mark the necessary items for making a kite from a selection of pictures.

subtopics.name: Responding to Social Situations
subtopics.summary: Students evaluate different responses to a scenario where a friend pushes someone during play.

subtopics.name: Project Work: Favorite Player
subtopics.summary: Students collect a picture of their favorite sports player and write a short description about them.

subtopics.name: Singing and Dancing
subtopics.summary: Students sing and dance along to a song about playing with friends.

subtopics.name: Adding Lines to a Song
subtopics.summary: Students creatively add new lines to the existing song, using prompts.

subtopics.name: Observing a Natural Scene
subtopics.summary: Students observe a detailed picture of a garden and identify various elements within it.

subtopics.name: Interpreting Dialogue from a Picture
subtopics.summary: Students interpret a conversation between a fly and a sunflower based on speech bubbles in an illustration.

subtopics.name: Listening for Story Premise
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a teacher's narration (from an appendix) to understand the fly's motivation for finding its name.

subtopics.name: Reading a Simple Narrative
subtopics.summary: Students read and understand a short story about a fly's journey.

subtopics.name: Identifying Characters and Plot
subtopics.summary: Students identify the main character (the fly) and the supporting characters (old woman, potter, barber, tailor, cobbler, Siddu) and follow the plot.

subtopics.name: Vocabulary of Professions
subtopics.summary: Students learn and recognize vocabulary related to common professions encountered in the story.

subtopics.name: Answering Comprehension Questions
subtopics.summary: Students answer multiple-choice and open-ended questions based on the details and events of the story.

subtopics.name: Sequencing Story Events
subtopics.summary: Students arrange a series of statements in the chronological order they occurred in the story.

subtopics.name: Identifying Professions and Actions
subtopics.summary: Students identify various professions and their associated tasks or products using a visual aid.

subtopics.name: Writing About Professions
subtopics.summary: Students practice writing descriptive sentences about different professions and what they do.

subtopics.name: Word Search
subtopics.summary: Students identify and circle hidden words from the chapter text within a letter grid.

subtopics.name: Dialogue Completion
subtopics.summary: Students complete speech bubbles for story characters based on a given scenario from the chapter's main story.

subtopics.name: Colouring Activity
subtopics.summary: Students colour an illustration of a fly.

subtopics.name: Community Helpers and Tools
subtopics.summary: Students identify and list tools used by various community helpers like tailors, cobblers, barbers, fishermen, and goldsmiths.

subtopics.name: Bang and a bang! Song/Poem
subtopics.summary: Students read, sing, and perform actions for a rhyme about a cobbler mending shoes.

subtopics.name: Reading the Story "Three little kittens"
subtopics.summary: Students read a multi-panel story about three kittens changing colours during their adventures.

subtopics.name: Animal Babies Vocabulary
subtopics.summary: Students learn the names of babies for common animals.

subtopics.name: Picture Observation
subtopics.summary: Observing a picture showing children carrying a large carrot.

subtopics.name: Reading a list and selecting relevant items for a specific recipe (carrot halwa)
subtopics.summary: Students demonstrate comprehension by identifying and listing ingredients required for a specific dish from a given list.

subtopics.name: Drawing and colouring favourite vegetables
subtopics.summary: Students express creativity by drawing and colouring vegetables they like.

subtopics.name: Developing writing skills by describing chosen vegetables
subtopics.summary: Students practice writing simple descriptive sentences about their drawn vegetables.

subtopics.name: Conducting a simple survey and collecting data from peers
subtopics.summary: Students learn to interact with peers to gather information for a simple survey.

subtopics.name: Recording collected information in a tabular format
subtopics.summary: Students practice organizing and writing collected data into a table.

subtopics.name: Singing a poem about various vegetables and their characteristics
subtopics.summary: Students engage in singing a poem that introduces different vegetables and their attributes.

subtopics.name: Participating in a song with actions that promotes themes of sharing, helping, and social interaction
subtopics.summary: Students learn and perform a song that encourages positive social behaviors like sharing and helping.

subtopics.name: Appu's Desire for Carrot Halwa
subtopics.summary: Appu attends Abhi's birthday party, enjoys carrot halwa, and asks his mother to make it daily.

subtopics.name: Mother's Advice on Healthy Eating
subtopics.summary: Appu's mother explains that eating too many sweets is unhealthy for teeth and overall health, emphasizing the need for healthy food.

subtopics.name: Suggestion to Grow Carrots
subtopics.summary: Appu's mother suggests growing fresh carrots in their garden so Appu can have them daily, but not as halwa, leading Appu to understand and feel happy.

subtopics.name: Textbook Development Team - 2012
subtopics.summary: Lists the members of the textbook development team.

subtopics.name: Textbook Publication Team
subtopics.summary: Lists the members of the textbook publication team.

subtopics.name: QR CODE TEAM
subtopics.summary: Identifies the team responsible for QR code integration.

subtopics.name: Evergreen Text Books Introduction
subtopics.summary: Explains the purpose of QR codes in the textbook for accessing additional learning content.

subtopics.name: Android Mobile Phone QR Code Usage Steps
subtopics.summary: Step-by-step guide on how to use an Android mobile phone to scan QR codes and view linked content.

subtopics.name: Computer QR Code Usage Steps
subtopics.summary: Step-by-step guide on how to use a computer to access content linked to QR codes.

subtopics.name: Personal Body Safety Rules
subtopics.summary: Illustrations and text conveying rules about not touching private parts in front of others and seeking help if touched inappropriately.

subtopics.name: Good Touch / Bad Touch
subtopics.summary: Visual cues and phrases like 'SAY NO! GO AWAY! TELL!!!' to help children understand and react to unwanted touch.

subtopics.name: Identifying and writing names of common vegetables
subtopics.summary: Students practice recognizing common vegetables from their images and writing their correct names.

subtopics.name: Practicing conversational English through role-play in a social context
subtopics.summary: Students develop their speaking and social interaction skills by role-playing a scenario in a sweet shop.

subtopics.name: Dialogue and Picture Analysis
subtopics.summary: Observing a picture of a boy and his mother discussing halwa, and reading their dialogue.

subtopics.name: Listening Comprehension: Carrot Halwa
subtopics.summary: Preparing for a listening activity about carrot halwa, with questions to be answered after listening to the teacher.

subtopics.name: Appu's Carrot Garden
subtopics.summary: Reading the beginning of the story about Appu planting and trying to pull carrots.

subtopics.name: Story Illustration - Appu Pulling Carrot
subtopics.summary: Observing an illustration depicting Appu and friends pulling carrots, with Appu struggling with a large one.

subtopics.name: Meena and Abhi Help
subtopics.summary: Reading the part of the story where Meena and Abhi join Appu to pull the carrot, but they are still unsuccessful.

subtopics.name: Story Illustration - Multiple Friends Pulling
subtopics.summary: Observing illustrations showing Meena joining Appu, and then Abhi joining them, all trying to pull the large carrot.

subtopics.name: Snoopy Joins the Pull
subtopics.summary: Reading the final part of the story where Snoopy helps, and the carrot is finally pulled out.

subtopics.name: Story Illustration - Carrot Pulled Out
subtopics.summary: Observing illustrations showing Snoopy joining the group to pull the carrot, and then the group celebrating their success with the pulled carrot.

subtopics.name: Carrot Halwa Celebration
subtopics.summary: Reading the conclusion of the story where Appu's mother makes halwa from the carrot, and everyone enjoys it.

subtopics.name: Story Illustration - Eating Halwa
subtopics.summary: Observing an illustration of the children and Snoopy enjoying carrot halwa made by Appu's mother.

subtopics.name: True or False
subtopics.summary: Answering true or false questions based on the story 'Together We Pull'.

subtopics.name: Group Discussion: Carrot Food Items
subtopics.summary: Engaging in a group discussion to brainstorm different food items that can be prepared with carrots, and presenting ideas to the class.

exercises.text: Look at the picture of children on a toy train at the zoo and answer: 1. Why are the children looking happy? 2. What animals do you see in the picture?
Look at the picture of a boy running after a van and answer: 1. Where are the children going? 2. What is the boy saying? Will he catch the van? 3. What is he taking with him?
Listen to the teacher about the boy (Abhi) and answer: 1. Why did Abhi get up early? 2. Who is Sweety? 3. Why did Abhi not take Sweety with him?
Identify and circle the words 'tiger', 'camel', 'monkey', 'kangaroo', 'jug', 'lion', 'xerox', 'parrot' in the pictures of the 'ZOO' (from previous pages).
Look at the picture of the 'ZOO' and write the names of animals, birds, and things in the provided A-Z table. Some are done for you (e.g., 'animal', 'fruit', 'nest', 'yoyo').
Sing and dance to the 'Dancing time' rhyme, which associates alphabet groups (ABCD, EFGH, etc.) with actions (dancing, hopping, learning, playing, story, writing, jolly).
Join the dots from A to Z. Colour the picture and write the letters from A to Z in capitals and small letters in the tables given below.
Read the names of the animals given below. (Cloud with names: elephant, tiger, lion, cat, bear, peacock, rat, zebra, monkey, fish)
Look at the pictures on pages 4 and 5 and write the names of the animals that are not given in the above cloud. Write them in the box given below.
Write the names of animals and birds and their first letter in the boxes and colour them. One is done for you. (Section 1: ANIMALS - bear, rabbit, lion, fish, giraffe, monkey)
We see different animals and birds in the zoo. Write their names in the boxes. (Mind map activity with 'animals and birds in the zoo' in the center)
Read the story 'Abhi goes to Zoo' about Abhi catching the van to the zoo.
Look at the picture of Abhi and his classmates at the zoo, observing various animals like elephant, zebra, rabbit, snake, and parrot.

Look at the picture of the zoo entrance and animals, then answer: 1. What do you see in the picture? 2. What are the children doing? 3. What is the natural home of wild animals? 4. What kind of things do animals need to be happy?

Sing, dance and add lines. Just like this... (Song lyrics provided: The monkeys are jumping, Just like this, just like this. The peacocks are dancing, Just like this, just like this. The elephants are stamping, Just like this, just like this.)
Add more lines to the song. (Prompts for frogs, birds, and two blank lines)
Look at the pictures. Tell the story to your friends in the group and present it before the class. (Four-panel story: A mouse and a snake)

Look at the picture showing a family and a kitten, and answer the following questions: 1. What do you see in the picture? 2. Why is the kitten running? 3. Why are the boy and the girl running after the kitten?

Look at the picture showing Abhi talking to his grandfather and Meena talking to her mother, and answer the following questions: 1. What is Abhi asking his grandfather? 2. What is his grandfather saying? 3. What is Meena asking her mother? 4. What is her mother saying?
Listen carefully to your teacher about 'Sweety' (listening text is in Appendix-1) and answer the following questions: 1. Who is Sweety? 2. Where did Sweety go? 3. What will Abhi and Meena do now?

Tick (✓) the correct answer in each of the following multiple-choice questions: 1. What is the name of the boy in the story? (Mohan / Shekhar / Abhi) 2. Who is Abhi's sister? (Meena / Rani / Latha) 3. Where was the kitten? (in the tree / in the chair / under the table)
Tick (✓) the boxes corresponding to the places where Abhi and Meena looked for Sweety, choosing from: cot, bag, cupboard, table, chair, tree.

Answer the following questions: 1. What is the name of Abhi's pet? 2. Do you have a pet? What is its name? 3. Abhi's pet is a kitten. What is your pet? 4. 'Abhi, look! Sweety is in the tree.' Who said this? 5. Why do you think Sweety didn't jump down from the tree?
Sweety is in a tree. It is a magic tree. It gives any fruit you want. Draw your favourite fruit on the tree and colour it.
Write the names of other fruits that you may love to have in the provided grid.
Look at the given animal pictures (cat, goat, donkey, cow, horse, pig, dog, sheep). Find their names in the letter grid and circle them. One example (cat) is done for you.

Look at the pictures of mothers and their babies. Name them. Choose the names of the babies from the box. Colour the mother and her baby with the same colour. (Pictures include: Duck, Cat, Buffalo, Dog, Hen with their respective babies. Word box: kitten, duckling, calf, puppy, chick)
Look at Abhi's dress. Do you like it? Oh! It has no colours. Colour the dress.
Write the names of the colours you know (using a mind map diagram with 'Colours' in the center and empty boxes).
Given below are the steps to draw a cat. Follow the steps and draw a cat (4 step-by-step images provided, with an empty box for drawing).
Name your cat and write about it (empty lined box for writing).
Sing the song 'My little Sweety' (lyrics provided: My little Sweety / Black little Sweety / Jumping here, jumping there / Jumping here and there).
Add lines to the song (prompts given for 'My little Ducky' and 'My little Puppy' with descriptive words like 'White', 'Dancing', 'brown', 'playing').

Look at the picture of a busy street and answer questions: 1. What do you see in the picture? 2. Where does the bus go? 3. Where are the boy and his father going? 4. How can we keep ourselves safe in crowded places?
Look at the picture of three children talking, one saying 'I will not come to school tomorrow', and answer the question: Why will Siddu not come to school?

Listen to the teacher tell about the boy and his visit to his uncle's house (listening text in Appendix-I) and answer the questions: 1. Why is Siddu going to Hyderabad? 2. Should Siddu tell his teacher that he is not coming to school? Why?
After reading the initial part of the story, answer the question: Why do you think Siddu is excited?
After reading about the bus tyre burst, answer the questions: What sound did the tyre produce? What made Siddu sad? What will Siddu and his father do now?

Answer the following questions based on the story: 1. How did Siddu and his father reach the bus stop? 2. Why did the bus stop suddenly? 3. What was Siddu happy at his uncle's house? Why? 4. What feelings did Siddu have during his journey? 5. Name some feelings that you have felt and why?

Fill in each blank with the right answer from the bracket: 1. Siddu and his father reached the bus stand in __________ (a cart/an auto). 2. The passengers were getting into the __________ (bus/van). 3. Siddu was __________ to play with the puppies (happy/sad).
Siddu and his father are standing on the road. They see many vehicles on the road. Name them using a mind map, with 'a car' already provided as an example.
Name the vehicles which Siddu travelled by to reach his uncle's house.

Look at the vehicles in the picture and write the names of the places (air, water and road) where you generally see them. (Includes a table with columns 'S. No.', 'Name of the vehicle', 'Travels in air/on water/on road' and 'boat' as an example).
Draw the missing parts of the bus and colour the bus.
Say something about the bus to your friend.
Draw a car with the help of the steps given below. Colour your car and name it.
Say something about your car to your friend.
Look at the picture and find out how many cars can manage to come out from the maze. Draw lines to find out the answer.
Answer the following questions based on the maze: 1. How many cars are there in the maze? 2. How many cars can come out? 3. Which car comes out last?
Sing, dance and add lines. (Refers to the song 'The wheels on the bus go round and round...' and 'The engine on the train goes chuk, chuk, chuk...').
Now add lines... (Complete the song with 'The horn on the car goes peep, peep, peep, ____ all day long.' and 'The bell on the bicycle goes tring, tring, tring, ____ all day long.').
Talk about the pictures (Refers to four sequential images depicting a dog interacting with a glass of water).
Look at the picture and answer the questions given below. Questions: 1. Where is the boy going? 2. Why is the puppy following him?

Your teacher will tell you about Snoopy and Siddu. Listen carefully and answer the following. Questions: 1. Why did Siddu's mother tie Snoopy? 2. Was Snoopy happy? Why did he bark? 3. Where was Snoopy going? 4. Did he meet Siddu?

Read the following. Snoopy comes to school. Snoopy comes to school. He wants to see Siddu. (Story continues across pages 39-40 with text and images describing Snoopy's search for Siddu in different classes and finally finding him in Class-II).

Answer the following questions. 1. Which room does Snoopy go first? 2. Was Siddu happy to see Snoopy at school? 3. What will you do if your pet follows you to school? 4. Imagine what happened later in the story.

Choose the correct answer from the box. One has been done for you. 1. Why do the children in Siddu's class laugh? (Options: Snoopy sits near Siddu. / Snoopy is at school.) 2. Why does Snoopy go to school? (Options: To see Siddu. / To see the headmaster.) 3. Why is Snoopy sad? (Options: he does not see Siddu. / the children laughed.) 4. Where does Snoopy sit in the class? (Options: Beside Siddu. / Beside headmaster.)
Correct the following and write them. 1. Children gave chocolates to Snoopy. 2. Snoopy saw Siddu and felt very sad. 3. Siddu was in the headmaster's room.

Siddu is packing his school bag to go to school. Circle the things which he keeps in his bag and write their names. (Image shows various items like a plate, pencil, airplane, crayons, toothbrush, pen, book, comb, cricket bat, football, teddy bear, soap, and pencil box.)

Draw and colour. This is Siddu's school. Look at it. Do these: a. Colour the school building. (blue) b. Colour the bell. (yellow) c. Colour the trees. (green) d. Draw a flag on the flag post and colour it.
Fill in the blanks with personal information about your school, including its name, number of classrooms, number of teachers, class teacher's name, and friends' names.
Write the names of different places that you see in the school, using a mind map format with 'School' at the center and 'playground' as an example.
Read the dialogue between Snoopy, Sweety, and a parrot about their abilities to catch thieves or rats.
In pairs, ask and answer 'Can you...?' questions using actions like swim, dance, laugh, paint, sing, and ride a bicycle, ticking 'Yes, I can' or 'No, I can't'.
List six other food and drink items that Snoopy might eat, besides biscuits, based on the provided image.
Tick the emoji that best represents how you would feel if your friend's pet came to school, choosing from happy, sad, fear, surprised, or angry.
Sing and dance along to the poem 'Come, little children...'.
Complete the poem 'Come, little children...' by adding new rhyming lines based on given cues like 'paint and draw', 'read and write', 'rhyme and mime', and 'learn and do'.
Read the short story titled 'A mistake' about Rahim and Razia realizing it's Sunday.
What do you see in the picture?
What do you see in the picture?
What will the boy do now?
Why did Abhi jump from the bed?
What did Abhi's mother say?
What do you like to do when it rains?
Where is it raining?
It's raining and you want to go out. How will you go?
What are the boys floating?
What other things are the children doing?
Who else are playing with the children?
Do you like playing in the rain?
How do you enjoy in the rain?
Why didn't mother allow Meena to go out in the rain?
What idea, do you think, Meena gets?
Where is Meena in the picture?
Why is Meena not jumping or running?
Why is Meena not wet?

Fill in the blanks. Choose the right word from the brackets. (1. The road is full of ________ (water/vehicles). 2. There are water droplets on ________ (grass/fruits). 3. Meena is in the ________ (verandah/kitchen). 4. Children are ________ (happy/sad). 5. Abhi and Siddu are playing with ________ (paper boats/toy train).)
Why are the children happy?
How does Meena not get wet?
What things do you use to protect yourself from rain?
What are the children doing in the picture given below? Do you like it? Why?

Talk to your friends and find out how many of them did the following. Put a tick (✓) mark. Activities include: get wet in the rain?, make paper boats?, touch a frog?, make kites?, fly kites?, splash rain water?, jump in the rain water?

Read the story: One day Meena forgot her umbrella at school. While going home in the evening it started raining. She walked in the rain and reached home in wet clothes. Then, sit in groups and complete the conversation between Meena and her mother. Role play it. The conversation starts with: Mother: Where is ___________? Meena: At ___________ Mother: Why did you leave it there? Meena: There was no ___________ when I started. Mother: ___________ Meena: ___________

Write any six words from the list given in the circle (rain, umbrella, boat, jump, window, mother, catch, children, grass, water, road). Put only one word in one box. Your teacher will read out any six words one by one. Listen and put a cross (x) if the word she/he calls out is in the box. The one who crosses out all the words first says, 'I am the winner.'

Letters with numbers are given in the box. Pick up the letter under each number and replace the numbers by writing it in the blanks and read. One is done for you. Example: Meena is playing in the water. (16 12 1 25 9 14 7). Sentences to complete: 1. Mother is making ___________ (19 23 5 5 20 19). 2. We need an ___________ in rain. (21 13 2 18 5 12 12 1). 3. Abhi likes to wear a ___________ (18 1 9 14 3 15 1 20). An alphabet key (a-z with numbers 1-26) is provided.
Look at the picture and answer the questions given below: 1. Where are the children playing? 2. What are they playing? 3. Who will catch the ball?
Look at the picture and answer the questions given below: 1. What are the children doing? 2. What games are they playing?
Your teacher will sing a song, 'Here we play...' Listen to it and answer the questions: 1. What games do you play with your friends? 2. Do you like to play inside the house or outside? Give reasons.
Answer questions related to the story: 1. What is the sound? 2. Where is the ball now? 3. Who do you think will try to catch/stop the ball?
Answer questions related to Teja's actions: 1. Why did Siddu ask Teja to throw the ball? 2. Why did Teja not throw the ball? 3. What do you think Teja picked up from the ground?
Answer questions related to the coin's discovery: 1. What did Teja pick up? 2. Whose coin was it? 3. If you found a five-rupee coin in your classroom, what would you do?
Answer questions related to the coin's ownership and Manu's sadness: 1. Why didn't Teja want to give the coin? 2. Why is Manu crying? 3. Why is it not correct to take somebody else's coin?

Choose the correct answer for story-based questions: 1. Who ran after the ball? (Abhi, Manu, Teja) 2. Who shouted at Teja to throw the ball quickly? (Rahim, Siddu, Abhi, Mary) 3. What did Teja pick up? (a 5 rupee coin, a ball, Sweety, a chocolate)
Answer the following questions based on the story: 1. Why was Manu crying? 2. Why did Manu thank his friends? 3. Do you play cricket? Are you a batsman or a bowler?
Write the names of different games that children play.
Look at the following pictures of playthings. Write the names of the things we need to play cricket in the box given below.
Draw two things that you like to play with.
Write a few sentences about one of them.
Work in pairs. Abhi is making a kite. His friend Siddu comes there. Role play their talk in pairs. Complete their conversation.
Look at the following pictures and put a tick (✓) on the things that we need to make a kite.

You are playing with your friends. One of your friends pushes someone on to the ground. What will you do? Put (✓) or (✗) for the given options: 1. You laugh at him. 2. You just go away from there. 3. Call the teacher. 4. Call other friends. 5. Help him to stand up. 6. You tell your friend that pushing others is bad.
Collect and paste the picture of your favourite player here and write about him/her.
Sing, dance and add lines to the song 'Here we play...' with prompts like 'run' and 'swing'.
Answer the question: What do you see in the picture?
Answer the question: Do you have gardens in your place?
Answer the question: Name any five flowers you know.
Answer the question based on the picture: Have you ever seen a sunflower?
Answer the question based on the picture: What are the sunflower and the fly talking about?
Answer the question after listening to the teacher: What will the fly do to know its name?
Answer the question after listening to the teacher: Why does the fly want to know its name?
Choose the correct answer: Whom did the fly meet first? (Options: potter, old woman, cobbler, barber)
Choose the correct answer: What was the barber doing? (Options: trimming, making shoes, stitching, playing)
Choose the correct answer: Whom did the fly call uncle? (Options: cobbler, tailor, barber, potter)
Answer the question: How did the fly know her name?
Answer the question: Who mends shoes?
Answer the question: You are lost in a fair. You have forgotten your address. How will you reach home?

Number the following statements in the order in which they appear in the story: The fly went to the barber. It saw the children playing in the ground. The fly was in the garden. It went to the cobbler. Siddu asked, "Hello fly, how are you?" The fly forgot her name.
Write sentences about Murali, Gangaiah, Somaiah, and Ranganna, describing their professions and what they do, based on the provided wheel diagram and example.
Find the words from the grid. Some of the words in the text are coloured. They are hidden in the grid below. Identify and circle them. One has been done for you.
What are they talking about? The fly went to Snoopy and Sweety. It said, 'What is my name?' Write in the bubbles what they said.
Colour the fly.

Project work: Work in groups and collect the following information from different people. Write items which they use in their work. One is done for you. (Includes a table for Tailor, Cobbler, Barber, Fisherman, Goldsmith).
Sing and dance. (Includes the poem 'Bang and a bang!' about a cobbler).
Read the story 'Three little kittens' (presented in 9 panels with accompanying text).
1. How many boys and girls are there in the picture? 2. What are the children carrying? 3. What do you think, they will do with the carrot?

Read the given list of items (sugar, bittergourd, carrot, salt, oil, ghee, stove, water, milk, a box of matches, spoon, tamarind, bowl, dry fruits, etc.) and pick out the things needed to make halwa, then list them in the provided blanks.
Draw and name any two vegetables that you like, colour your pictures, and write about them in the provided lines.
Ask your friends in the class about their favourite sweet and write their name and favourite sweet in the given table (S. No., Name of your friend, his/her favourite sweet).
Sing and enjoy the poem about tomatoes, beans, brinjal, potatoes, onions, and carrots.
Sing, dance, and add lines to the song about sharing toys, toffees, helping each other, walking together, greeting, and living.

Students are expected to understand and discuss the personal body safety rules depicted in the illustrations, including identifying private parts, appropriate help with dressing, and actions to take if someone touches them inappropriately, using the visual cues and phrases provided.
Identify the vegetables shown in the pictures and write their names by choosing from the given word box (cabbage, radish, brinjal, turnip, beetroot, carrot, tomato, potato).
Work in groups to role play a conversation between Meena, Abhi, Siddu, and a shopkeeper in a sweet shop, discussing what the shopkeeper will tell them.
1. Who are the people in the picture? 2. The boy likes halwa. What do you like?
1. Have you ever tasted halwa? What is it made of? 2. If you want to cook carrot halwa, what ingredients do you need?

1. What happened to one of the carrot plants? 2. Who helped Appu in pulling the carrot out? 3. Have you ever tasted carrot halwa? Do you like it? How does it taste? 4. What food do you like to eat the most?
1. Appu calls Meena for help. 2. Snoopy does not help Appu. 3. Carrot grows above the soil. 4. Amma makes carrot halwa.
What different food items can we prepare with carrots? Discuss with your friends. Write your ideas on a chart and present them before the whole class.

topics.topic_name: Introduction to the Zoo and Observation Skills
topics.concept_summary: Introduces the concept of a zoo through visual observation and basic questions, including a listening comprehension activity.

subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture of children on a toy train at a zoo with various animals.
Students answer questions based on their observation of the zoo scene.
Students observe a picture of a boy running after a school van.
Students listen to a story about a boy named Abhi and answer related questions.

topics.topic_name: Abhi goes to Zoo Story and Comprehension
topics.concept_summary: A short story about Abhi's journey to the zoo with his classmates, followed by visual comprehension activities.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Reading the Story 'Abhi goes to Zoo'
subtopics.summary: Students read a short narrative about Abhi's trip to the zoo.
Students look at pictures depicting Abhi and his classmates inside the zoo, observing various animals.
Students answer questions based on the visual content of the zoo pictures.

topics.topic_name: Zoo Vocabulary, Alphabet Practice, and Rhyme
topics.concept_summary: Activities focused on identifying zoo-related vocabulary, practicing alphabet recognition and writing, and a singing/dancing rhyme.

subtopics.summary: Students identify and circle specific words corresponding to objects or animals found in the zoo pictures.
Students write names of animals, birds, and things from the zoo pictures into an alphabetical table.
Students engage in a singing and dancing activity based on an alphabet rhyme.

topics.concept_summary: Students practice alphabet sequencing by joining dots from A to Z, identify and write capital and small letters, and color a picture.

subtopics.summary: Students connect dots labeled with letters from A to Z in sequence to reveal a picture.
Students write capital and small letters of the English alphabet in provided tables.
Students color the picture revealed by the dot-to-dot activity.

topics.topic_name: Identifying and Naming Zoo Animals and Birds
topics.concept_summary: Students read animal names, identify animals not present in a given list, write names of animals and birds, associate them with their first letters, and classify them.

subtopics.summary: Students read a list of animal names presented in a cloud graphic.
Students recall and write the names of animals seen in earlier pages that were not listed in the provided cloud.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Animal/Bird Name and First Letter Association
subtopics.summary: Students write the names of various animals and birds, along with their corresponding first letters (capital and small), and color their pictures.
Students categorize and write the names of different animals and birds found in a zoo using a mind map structure.

topics.topic_name: Animal Action Song and Creative Writing
topics.concept_summary: Students engage in singing and dancing to a song about animal actions and are encouraged to add new lines to the song, fostering creativity and vocabulary.

subtopics.summary: Students sing and dance along to a song about animals and their actions.
Students create and add new lines to the existing song, describing other animal actions.

topics.topic_name: Picture Story Narration: A Mouse and a Snake
topics.concept_summary: Students interpret a four-panel picture story about a mouse and a snake and narrate it orally to their peers, developing visual comprehension and storytelling skills.

subtopics.summary: Students analyze a sequence of images to understand the events and plot of a story.
Students verbally recount the story depicted in the pictures to their classmates.

topics.concept_summary: Students observe pictures and answer questions to introduce the characters and setting of the story 'Where are you, Sweety?' and engage in a listening activity.

subtopics.summary: Students analyze an image of a family and a kitten, answering questions to infer the initial plot.
Students observe images of characters interacting and answer questions, followed by a listening activity to understand the context of 'Sweety'.

topics.topic_name: The Story: Where are you, Sweety?
topics.concept_summary: Students read a short story about Abhi and Meena searching for their kitten, Sweety, and their father helping to retrieve it from a tree.

subtopics.summary: Students read about Abhi and Meena looking for Sweety inside their house, specifically under the cot and on the cupboard.
Students read about Abhi and Meena finding Sweety in a tree in the garden and their father, Mohan, helping to bring her down.

topics.concept_summary: Students answer various questions to demonstrate their understanding of the story, identify key details, and relate the story to their own experiences with pets.

subtopics.summary: Students answer multiple-choice questions to recall specific details from the story, such as character names and the kitten's location.
Students identify and mark the places where Abhi and Meena searched for Sweety.
Students answer open-ended questions about the story and reflect on their own experiences with pets.

topics.concept_summary: Students engage in creative activities involving drawing, coloring, and writing, inspired by elements from the story.

subtopics.summary: Students draw and color their favorite fruit on a depicted 'magic tree' where Sweety was found.
Students write the names of other fruits they enjoy.

topics.concept_summary: Students identify various animals and locate their names within a word search grid, enhancing their vocabulary and recognition skills.

subtopics.summary: Students identify common animals from pictures and find their names hidden in a letter grid.

topics.concept_summary: This topic introduces students to the names of common animal babies and provides exercises for identifying and colouring them. It also includes activities to practice identifying and writing colour names.

subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify various animal mothers and their corresponding babies through pictures and a word box.
Students practice colouring skills by matching colours to animal pairs and a boy's dress. They also recall and write names of colours they know.
Students engage in a mind-mapping activity to list and write down various colour names.

topics.concept_summary: This topic guides students through a step-by-step process of drawing an animal and encourages them to name their drawing and write a short description about it, fostering creativity and writing skills.

subtopics.summary: Students follow a sequence of steps to draw a cat, developing their fine motor skills and artistic ability.
Students are prompted to give a name to their drawn cat and write a few sentences about it, encouraging imaginative thinking and basic writing practice.

topics.concept_summary: This topic presents a simple song about 'My little Sweety' and encourages students to extend the song by adding new lines about other animals, promoting listening, singing, and creative writing skills.

subtopics.summary: Students learn and sing a short song about a kitten, developing listening and oral language skills.
Students are encouraged to create and add new verses to the existing song, using given prompts for other animals like a ducky and a puppy, enhancing their creative writing and rhyming abilities.

topics.concept_summary: This topic introduces Siddu and his father's journey to his uncle's house, encountering various modes of transport and challenges along the way, culminating in his arrival and joy at seeing puppies.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Observing and Inferring from a Scene
subtopics.summary: Students observe a detailed picture of a crowded street and answer questions that require visual interpretation and basic inference about the scene and characters' intentions.
Students predict why Siddu won't come to school based on a speech bubble and then listen to a story segment (read by the teacher) to answer specific comprehension questions about Siddu's trip.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Reading the Beginning of the Story
subtopics.summary: Students read the initial part of the narrative, learning about Siddu's excitement and the start of his journey to his uncle's house by cart and then bus.
Students read about an unexpected event during the journey – a bus tyre burst – and Siddu's subsequent sadness and the family's attempt to find alternative transport.
Students read about Siddu and his father continuing their journey, encountering more challenges with transport, and finally reaching the bustling city of Hyderabad.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Reading About the Arrival and Joy
subtopics.summary: Students read about Siddu's arrival at his uncle's house, being greeted by his relatives, and his immense happiness upon discovering and playing with puppies.

topics.concept_summary: This topic contains various exercises designed to test comprehension, vocabulary, and writing skills based on the story 'A Visit to Uncle's House'.
This topic introduces students to different types of vehicles, their modes of travel, and associated sounds and actions through identification, drawing, maze activities, and a song.

subtopics.summary: Students identify various vehicles (boat, car, bus, airplane) and categorize them by where they travel (air, water, road).
Students practice drawing and coloring a bus and a car, following step-by-step instructions for the car.
Students solve a maze involving cars, count the number of cars, and determine which cars can exit and which exits last.
Students sing a song about vehicles (bus, train, car, bicycle), their movements, and sounds, and add new lines to the song.

topics.concept_summary: This topic focuses on developing oral storytelling skills by describing a sequence of events depicted in pictures.

subtopics.summary: Students observe a series of four pictures showing a dog's interaction with a glass of water and narrate the story.

topics.concept_summary: Students analyze pictures related to Siddu and Snoopy going to school and answer questions, including a listening comprehension activity.

subtopics.summary: Students observe images and answer questions based on visual cues and inference.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Listening comprehension about Snoopy and Siddu
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story or dialogue (from Appendix-1) and answer questions based on the audio.

topics.topic_name: The Story: Snoopy Comes to School
topics.concept_summary: The main story about Snoopy searching for Siddu in school, visiting different classes, and finally finding him.

subtopics.summary: Students read the narrative text about Snoopy's adventure at school.
Students follow the sequence of events in the story, such as Snoopy visiting different classes.
Students recognize characters like Snoopy and Siddu and understand their feelings throughout the story.

topics.concept_summary: Exercises to test understanding of the story, correct statements, and identify school-related items.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Answering direct questions about the story
subtopics.summary: Students answer open-ended and inferential questions based on the story content.
Students select the appropriate answer from given choices to demonstrate story comprehension.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Correcting false statements from the story
subtopics.summary: Students identify incorrect statements related to the story and rewrite them correctly.
Students recognize items typically found in a school bag and write their names.

topics.concept_summary: An art activity involving colouring a school scene and drawing a flag.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Drawing an element in an image
subtopics.summary: Students draw a flag on a flag post within the school image.

topics.concept_summary: Activities focused on describing one's school environment, including personal details and identifying various places within the school.

subtopics.summary: Students fill in blanks to provide personal information about their school.
Students identify and list different areas or facilities found within a school.

topics.concept_summary: Learning to ask and answer questions about personal abilities using 'Can you?' in a conversational context.

Students practice asking and responding to questions about various actions and abilities.

topics.concept_summary: Activities related to identifying different food items and expressing various emotions.

subtopics.summary: Students list various food and drink items based on visual cues.
Students identify and select the appropriate emotional response to a given scenario.

topics.concept_summary: Engaging with a song/poem through singing, dancing, and creative writing by adding new lines.

subtopics.summary: Students participate in singing and dancing to a short poem.
Students extend a poem by adding new rhyming lines based on provided prompts.

topics.concept_summary: Reading and comprehending a short story about a common everyday mistake.

subtopics.summary: Students read a narrative text to understand the plot and characters.
Students grasp the context and humorous resolution of the story.

topics.topic_name: Look at the picture and answer the question given below.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Visual observation of a rainy street

subtopics.subtopic_name: Listening comprehension of a rainy day story

subtopics.subtopic_name: Personal reflection on rainy day activities

subtopics.summary: Students reflect on their own enjoyment of rain and how they like to play in it.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Comprehension of character actions and motivations
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions about Meena's feelings, her mother's reasons, and Meena's solution to play in the rain.

Students act out the completed conversation in groups.

topics.topic_name: I Am The Winner! Word Game

Students listen to words read by the teacher and identify if they have written them down, then cross them out.

topics.concept_summary: Students decode sentences by replacing numbers with corresponding letters from a provided alphabet key.

subtopics.summary: Students use a number-to-letter key to fill in blanks and complete sentences.
Students read the sentences after successfully decoding them.

topics.concept_summary: Students draw and color pictures of two items used during the rainy season and discuss them.

subtopics.summary: Students draw two objects commonly used in the rainy season.
Students color their drawings of rainy season items.
Students talk about the items they drew and colored.

topics.topic_name: Rain on the Green Grass Song

Students compose and add new lines to the 'Rain on the green grass' poem.

topics.concept_summary: Students observe pictures of children playing various games and answer questions based on their observations, including a listening activity related to games.

subtopics.summary: Students listen to a song about playing games and answer questions related to their own experiences with games.
