subtopics.summary: Students read the initial part of the story, introducing characters playing cricket and the first event.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Reading about Teja finding the coin
subtopics.summary: Students read about Teja's actions after the ball is hit, leading to the discovery of a five-rupee coin.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Reading about the coin's ownership and Manu crying
subtopics.summary: Students read the dialogue among the children regarding the coin's ownership and the discovery of Manu crying.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Reading the resolution of the story
subtopics.summary: Students read how the coin is identified as Manu's and returned, concluding the story.

topics.concept_summary: Exercises designed to assess students' comprehension of the story 'Here is your coin!' through multiple-choice and short-answer questions, followed by an activity to list different games.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Multiple choice questions on the story
subtopics.summary: Students answer multiple-choice questions based on the events and characters in the story.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Short answer questions on the story
subtopics.summary: Students provide short written answers to questions requiring deeper understanding of the story's plot and themes.
Students recall and write the names of various games children play, applying their knowledge of the chapter's theme.

topics.concept_summary: Students identify various playthings, specifically cricket equipment, and express their personal preferences by drawing and writing about their favorite play items.

subtopics.summary: Students observe pictures of various playthings.
Students identify and list items specifically used for playing cricket from a collection of playthings.
Students draw two playthings they personally like to play with.
Students write a few sentences describing one of their drawn favorite playthings.

topics.concept_summary: Students engage in a role-play conversation about making a kite and identify the materials needed for kite making.

subtopics.summary: Students role-play a conversation between two friends about making a kite.
Students complete a given dialogue based on the context of making a kite.
Students identify and mark the necessary items for making a kite from a selection of pictures.

subtopics.summary: Students evaluate different responses to a scenario where a friend pushes someone during play.
Students collect a picture of their favorite sports player and write a short description about them.

topics.concept_summary: Students engage in singing and dancing to a given song and then creatively add new lines to the song.

subtopics.summary: Students sing and dance along to a song about playing with friends.
Students creatively add new lines to the existing song, using prompts.

topics.concept_summary: This topic introduces the chapter through picture observation and initial questions, setting the stage for the story about a fly searching for its name.

subtopics.summary: Students observe a detailed picture of a garden and identify various elements within it.
Students interpret a conversation between a fly and a sunflower based on speech bubbles in an illustration.
Students listen to a teacher's narration (from an appendix) to understand the fly's motivation for finding its name.

topics.concept_summary: This topic presents the narrative of a fly searching for its name, encountering various people engaged in different professions.

subtopics.summary: Students read and understand a short story about a fly's journey.
Students identify the main character (the fly) and the supporting characters (old woman, potter, barber, tailor, cobbler, Siddu) and follow the plot.
Students learn and recognize vocabulary related to common professions encountered in the story.

topics.topic_name: Comprehension and Application of Story Concepts
topics.concept_summary: This topic includes activities designed to test comprehension of the story and apply learned vocabulary about professions.

subtopics.summary: Students answer multiple-choice and open-ended questions based on the details and events of the story.
Students arrange a series of statements in the chronological order they occurred in the story.
Students identify various professions and their associated tasks or products using a visual aid.
Students practice writing descriptive sentences about different professions and what they do.

topics.concept_summary: This section includes a word search activity to find words from the chapter text and a dialogue completion exercise based on the story characters.

subtopics.summary: Students identify and circle hidden words from the chapter text within a letter grid.
Students complete speech bubbles for story characters based on a given scenario from the chapter's main story.

subtopics.summary: Students colour an illustration of a fly.
Students identify and list tools used by various community helpers like tailors, cobblers, barbers, fishermen, and goldsmiths.

topics.topic_name: Rhyme and Movement: Bang and a bang!
topics.concept_summary: This section features a song/poem about a cobbler, encouraging students to sing and dance along.

subtopics.summary: Students read, sing, and perform actions for a rhyme about a cobbler mending shoes.

topics.concept_summary: This section presents a new story about three kittens and their adventures, followed by a vocabulary building sidebar on animal babies.

subtopics.summary: Students read a multi-panel story about three kittens changing colours during their adventures.
Students learn the names of babies for common animals.

topics.topic_name: Introduction to the Story / Picture Analysis
topics.concept_summary: Students observe a picture related to the chapter theme and answer questions based on their observation and interpretation.

subtopics.summary: Observing a picture showing children carrying a large carrot.

topics.topic_name: Introducing Carrot Halwa and Listening Comprehension
topics.concept_summary: Students observe a picture and dialogue about halwa, answer questions, and prepare for a listening activity about the boy and carrot halwa.

subtopics.summary: Observing a picture of a boy and his mother discussing halwa, and reading their dialogue.
Preparing for a listening activity about carrot halwa, with questions to be answered after listening to the teacher.

topics.topic_name: The Story: Appu Tries to Pull a Carrot
topics.concept_summary: Introduction to the story of Appu, who plants carrots and struggles to pull out a very large one, eventually calling for help.

subtopics.summary: Reading the beginning of the story about Appu planting and trying to pull carrots.
Observing an illustration depicting Appu and friends pulling carrots, with Appu struggling with a large one.

topics.topic_name: The Story: More Friends Join to Pull the Carrot
topics.concept_summary: The story continues with Appu asking Meena for help, and then Abhi joining them, but they still cannot pull out the giant carrot.

subtopics.summary: Reading the part of the story where Meena and Abhi join Appu to pull the carrot, but they are still unsuccessful.
Observing illustrations showing Meena joining Appu, and then Abhi joining them, all trying to pull the large carrot.

topics.concept_summary: The story concludes with Snoopy the dog joining the effort, and together, the group successfully pulls out the giant carrot.

subtopics.summary: Reading the final part of the story where Snoopy helps, and the carrot is finally pulled out.
Observing illustrations showing Snoopy joining the group to pull the carrot, and then the group celebrating their success with the pulled carrot.

topics.concept_summary: The story concludes with Appu's mother making carrot halwa from the giant carrot, which everyone enjoys. Followed by comprehension questions about the story.

subtopics.summary: Reading the conclusion of the story where Appu's mother makes halwa from the carrot, and everyone enjoys it.
Observing an illustration of the children and Snoopy enjoying carrot halwa made by Appu's mother.

topics.concept_summary: Activities to reinforce comprehension of the story and encourage creative thinking and discussion about carrots and food.

subtopics.summary: Answering true or false questions based on the story 'Together We Pull'.
Engaging in a group discussion to brainstorm different food items that can be prepared with carrots, and presenting ideas to the class.

topics.concept_summary: Students identify and name various vegetables from pictures, using a word bank.

topics.concept_summary: Students engage in a group role-play activity simulating a conversation in a sweet shop.

subtopics.summary: Students develop their speaking and social interaction skills by role-playing a scenario in a sweet shop.

topics.concept_summary: Students read a list of items and select the necessary ingredients for making carrot halwa, based on previous story context.

subtopics.summary: Students demonstrate comprehension by identifying and listing ingredients required for a specific dish from a given list.

topics.concept_summary: Students draw and colour their favourite vegetables and write descriptive sentences about them.

subtopics.summary: Students express creativity by drawing and colouring vegetables they like.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Developing writing skills by describing chosen vegetables
subtopics.summary: Students practice writing simple descriptive sentences about their drawn vegetables.

topics.concept_summary: Students conduct a simple survey among their friends to find out their favourite sweets and record the information in a table.

subtopics.summary: Students learn to interact with peers to gather information for a simple survey.

subtopics.summary: Students practice organizing and writing collected data into a table.

topics.concept_summary: Students sing poems about vegetables and participate in a song with actions about sharing and helping.

subtopics.subtopic_name: Singing a poem about various vegetables and their characteristics
subtopics.summary: Students engage in singing a poem that introduces different vegetables and their attributes.

subtopics.summary: Students learn and perform a song that encourages positive social behaviors like sharing and helping.

topics.topic_name: Chapter 8 Narrative Summary: Healthy Eating and Growing Carrots
topics.concept_summary: A narrative summary of Chapter 8, focusing on Appu's desire for carrot halwa after a birthday party, his mother's advice on the health implications of eating too many sweets, and the suggestion to grow fresh carrots in their garden for daily consumption. This reinforces themes of healthy eating and the origin of food.

subtopics.summary: Appu attends Abhi's birthday party, enjoys carrot halwa, and asks his mother to make it daily.
Appu's mother explains that eating too many sweets is unhealthy for teeth and overall health, emphasizing the need for healthy food.
Appu's mother suggests growing fresh carrots in their garden so Appu can have them daily, but not as halwa, leading Appu to understand and feel happy.

topics.concept_summary: Details the teams involved in the development and publication of the textbook, including the Textbook Development Team, Textbook Publication Team, and QR Code Team.

topics.concept_summary: Provides blank lined pages for students or teachers to write notes, observations, or additional information.
